title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A nonprofit live-arts archive. They need private OAC origins, signed season access, exact cache identity, range-aware recordings and adaptive segments, captions and transcripts, accessible controls, WAF protection, urgent purge, and bearer-leakage limits. Planned artifact: Deliver Private Performing-Arts Media Platform with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Oac Sourcearn Policy
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2391 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Oac Sourcearn Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, scope the cloudfront service principal to the exact distribution sourcearn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Oac Sourcearn Policy, keep Oac Sourcearn Policy connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
